author: @marcfruchtman9473
text: I think it is really cool how the magnetic poles can be "bent" around. I suppose that is why Halbach arrays work.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's path of least reluctance mate - funny how it works like that - always reminds me of path of lest resistance too

replies.author: @andrewsmithmilan1780
replies.text: Halbach arrays don't bend the field, they force it to be more focused and stronger.

replies.author: @marcfruchtman9473
replies.text: @andrewsmithmilan1780 Not sure why you think that is not "bending" the field.

replies.author: @andrewsmithmilan1780
replies.text:
@marcfruchtman9473 because it's not bending it. It's literally making it straighter, stronger, and more focused.
What Robert did in the video is bending the field, but that's not a halbach array.

replies.author: @marcfruchtman9473
replies.text: @andrewsmithmilan1780  Apologies, I did not mean to imply that what Robert was creating was a Halbach Array... You are correct that what Robert created is not a Halbach array. However, the way a Halbach array works actually orients the magnetic fields of the magnets in such a way that it actually bends the fields. You can search on "Halbach Array" and "Bend" and the term is very commonly associated with the way it operates.

author: @ThatTweedle
text:
this one is INGENIOUS!!!! how frugal!!!  I am confused about one thing tho, how was the first one wired?  You called it a 'heath robinson' arrangement?  I tried searching for things that had to with electricity and 'heath robinson' and all I could find was stuff about a cartoonist from ohio and some senate bill named after him in 2022 LOL
So... I've been trying to figure out how it would spin and I just cannot put it together in my head... Maybe its using AC and not DC? I just can't seem to make it clique.... Could you help me out with this :) Thanks

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: sorry mate i was a bit quick about that bit - the three coils on the motor are independent and fired one after the other with the motor controller you can wire in delta or y configuration it doesn't matter - y and delta only refers to how the coils are joined

replies.author: @ThatTweedle
replies.text: ​@ThinkingandTinkering ahhh so is there just one power supply and it goes into a motor controller board, and they that outputs to each of the three coils?

author: @gaspumprepairservice7009
text:
Just wow. Did you really say “thirty volts” or “thirteen”?
Either way, too cool. I am a bit surprised that you didn’t mention recovering the wire from the voice coil. ✌️

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: 30 and the coils in voice coils are very thin wire and very hard to handle the coils themselves are too small to get much of a voltage out of and you would have to form slugs to center them - much more trouble than most folks would go with
